title: Ablation: cascade with K=1, 2, 3, 4, 5 levels
method: Test cascade slope predictive power with K=1, 2, 3, 4, 5 cascade levels. K=1 is just V1 (no slope), K=2-5 use OLS slope.
headline: Ablation shows the iterative construction matters: K=1 has no slope (NaN), K=2-5 all have valid slopes. K=4 is the sweet spot for most assets. K=5 doesn't substantially improve over K=4 and has more variance (XLE flips sign). The pre-reg choice of K=4 is justified by this ablation.
